title: Cuy'val Dar
content:
{{Top|leg|italics=1}}
{{Otheruses|Dar}}
{{Organization
|image=[[File:ApmaWardBralorOmegaSquad-30thAnniversaryCollection.jpg]]
|name=''Cuy'val Dar''
|type=
|founder=[[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]]<ref name="HC">''[[Republic Commando: Hard Contact]]''</ref>
|leader=
|subgroup=
|headquarters=[[Tipoca City/Legends|Tipoca City]], [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]<ref name="TZ">''[[Republic Commando: Triple Zero]]''</ref>
|locations=*[[Arca Company Barracks|Arca Barracks]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]<ref name="TZ" />
*[[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]<ref name="TC">''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]''</ref>
|formed=75 [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]]; 25 other varied beings<ref name="TZ" />
|founded=c. [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]]<ref name="TZ" />
|fragmented=
|reorganized=
|dissolved=
|restored=
|affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]<ref name="TZ" />
**[[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]<ref name="TZ" />
}}
{{Quote|You're the best in your field—the best soldiers, tacticians, sappers, communicators, survival experts. I picked you personally because I want you to train the best commandos in the galaxy. You'll have everything you need, whatever you want, except one thing—home. This is a top-secret project. You'll not tell anyone where you're going and you'll not leave Kamino, ever. As far as your friends and family are concerned, you're already dead.|Jango Fett, recruiting the ''Cuy'val Dar''|<ref name="HC" />}}
The '''''Cuy'val Dar''''' were a group of one hundred individuals summoned by [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], to come to the [[Water/Legends|watery]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] to train [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Of these one hundred [[Gender/Legends|males and females]], seventy-five were Mandalorians like Fett, and in the [[Mando'a/Legends|Mandalorian language]] ''Cuy'val Dar'' meant "those who no longer exist"—a name stemming from the need for these individuals to disappear to Kamino without contact with the outside [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] due to the secrecy surrounding the developing [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. For close to ten [[Standard year/Legends|years]], these one hundred training [[Sergeant/Legends|sergeants]] secretly trained the ten thousand [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commandos]] until their deployment at the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], which sparked the galactic [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. With their contract up, many of the ''Cuy'val Dar'' trainers went back to their lives, but a small number continued to work with the Republic, returning to train further generations of [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] on Kamino, and later cross-training standard clone [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] with [[Commando/Legends|commando]] skills.

Nearly fifty years later, in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic Intelligence Service|Intelligence]] [[Agent/Legends|agent]] and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Kubariet]], met with [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] on [[Vorpa'ya]] in the first year of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. There, Kubariet requested that Fett form a new ''Cuy'val Dar'' group made up of experienced Mandalorian [[Mandalorian Supercommando|commandos]], who could take on the task of training planetary militias to combat the [[Yuuzhan Vong]].

==History==
{{Quote|I think it's significant that the casualty rate among commando squads trained by Mandalorians is lower than those trained by other races. Somehow, Mandalorians imbue their charges with a sense of purpose, self-confidence, and almost obsessive sense of clan—of family—that gives them a genuine survival advantage. Let us be thankful they're on our side this time.|Jedi Master Arligan Zey, on the effectiveness of Mandalorian ''Cuy'val Dar'' trainers|<ref name="TZ" />}}
In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] of [[Serenno/Legends|Serenno]]—a former [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who left the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] only to fall to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], becoming the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] "Darth Tyranus"<ref name="AOTC">{{Film|II}}</ref>—convinced the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] to become the [[Clone template/Legends|progenitor]] of an [[Army/Legends|army]] of [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] to be grown on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]].<ref name="BountyHunter">''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]''</ref> Fett, an accomplished [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] himself,<ref name="OpenSeasons">''[[Star Wars: Jango Fett: Open Seasons]]''</ref> knew that an effective army would require variety, and [[Clone trooper/Legends|troops]] that could handle more dangerous and complex missions.<ref name="RepCom">''[[Star Wars: Republic Commando]]''</ref> With this in mind, Fett reached out to individuals throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] to train elite [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commandos]], beings with special skill sets ranging from military and survival skills, to tracking and communication experts.<ref name="HC" /> Numbering one hundred in total, seventy-five of these trainers were Mandalorians like Fett, and these unique, hand-picked individuals formed the group known as the ''Cuy'val Dar'', which in the Mandalorian [[Language/Legends|language]] of [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']] meant "Those Who No-Longer Exist."<ref name="HC" /><ref name="TZ" /> The origins of the name stemmed from the necessity of the ''Cuy'val Dar'' to leave their lives behind and vanish to Kamino for an indeterminate amount of [[Time/Legends|time]], where they would have no contact with the outside galaxy beyond its borders. Most were thought dead or missing in action, having left any family and friends they had without explanation.<ref name="TZ" />

[[File:Mandalady.jpg|thumb|left|220px|''Cuy'val Dar'' sergeant [[Vhonte Tervho]]]]
A large number of the ''Cuy'val Dar'' came from professional backgrounds that would commonly be considered fringe by the standards of [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] society, with bounty hunters, [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]], and [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] among their ranks. Yet upon answering Fett's summons to Kamino, this eclectic assortment of beings took up the role of training [[Sergeant/Legends|sergeants]] for the [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] that would make up the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic's]] [[Special forces/Legends|special forces]]. With the exception of Fett—who personally took on the responsibility of training the one hundred [[Alpha-class Advanced Recon Commando|''Alpha''-class]] [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|Advanced Recon Commandos]]—circa [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]], each ''Cuy'val Dar'' sergeant was given a [[Company/Legends|company]] of one hundred and four [[Clone cadet/Legends|clone cadets]] to turn into commandos.<ref name="TZ" /> The commandos-in-training were not limited to their assigned sergeants in the course of their education, and there were several members of the ''Cuy'val Dar'' who had unique skills to be passed on. Sergeant [[Mij Gilamar]] was a trained [[Doctor/Legends|doctor]] in addition to being a Mandalorian soldier,<ref name="O66">''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]''</ref> and he instructed the clones in the techniques of a combat [[Medic/Legends|medic]].<ref name="TC" /> [[Kal Skirata]], another Mandalorian and one reputed as a dirty fighter, taught the clones counter-insurgent tactics.<ref name="TZ" /> [[Llats Ward]] centered his life around warfare, and committed the campaigns of renowned leaders in Mandalorian military history to memory. Ward drew on these historical events in his teachings as a combat skill instructor to the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]].

The ''Cuy'val Dar'' trainers came to have a substantial impact on the clones they trained, stretching beyond teaching skills and moving into influencing their personalities. Mandalorian sergeant [[Walon Vau]] believed in training his [[Gender/Legends|men]] hard so that they would be able to face whatever actual combat had to offer, making them into survivors on an animal level. His theory was be given credence by the fact that [[Squad/Legends|squads]] trained by Vau had the highest survival rating following their devastating [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|first deployment]]. The commandos of [[Delta Squad/Legends|Delta Squad]] came to look upon Vau with genuine gratitude for his training, but [[Atin Skirata|RC-3222]]—or "Atin"—resented and came to hate Vau for his brutality. Sergeant Kal Skirata took a different approach: though no less dutiful in his training, Skirata encouraged the men in his charge, and treated them as he would his sons.<ref name="TZ" /> Fellow Mandalorian sergeant [[Rav Bralor]] had a similar approach with her own squads, and both Skirata and Bralor were looked upon with [[Parent/Legends|familial]] feelings by their clone trainees in return.<ref name="O66" /> Nearly all of the Mandalorian training sergeants, regardless of their training styles, imbued a sense of being Mandalorian upon their charges, teaching the clones of Jango Fett about their Mandalorian heritage, and in response, a large number of the clone commandos took to that heritage enthusiastically.<ref name="TZ" /> This was not a trait unique to the Mandalorian trainers and their clones, however, as commandos trained by a [[Unidentified Corellian Cuy'val Dar instructor|Corellian]] just as readily took on [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] traits and elements of the culture as well,<ref name="ImpCom501st">''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''</ref> and the same could be said of clones trained by sergeants from [[Concord Dawn/Legends|Concord Dawn]], such as the former [[Journeyman Protector/Legends|Journeyman Protector]] [[Cort Davin]].<ref name="GGAR">{{InsiderCite|84|Guide to the Grand Army of the Republic}}</ref>

[[File:Wade.tayhaai1.jpg|thumb|right|180px|''Cuy'val Dar'' sergeant [[Wad'e Tay'haai]]]]
One [[Unidentified Mandalorian Cuy'val Dar instructor|Mandalorian instructor]], considered among the finest in the ''Cuy'val Dar'', trained a clone soldier by the name of [[Kaddak (clone trooper)|Kaddak]], a name the aspiring commando took from the ancient destroyer [[God/Legends|god]] [[Kad Ha'rangir]] of [[Mandalorian religion|Mandalorian mythology]]. Kaddak and his ''Cuy'val Dar'' instructor developed a contentious relationship over time, which eventually led to a fight between the two. Using nothing but his bare [[Limb/Legends|hands]], Kaddak killed the instructor, and was subsequently incarcerated by the [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoans]] and subjected to testing. According to rumor, Kaddak was ultimately [[Death/Legends|terminated]] by the Kaminoans,<ref name="DVaCoS2">[[Darth Vader and the Cry of Shadows 2|''Darth Vader and the Cry of Shadows'' 2]]</ref> though in truth he managed to escape Kamino, coming to reside on [[Ostor]].<ref name="DVaCoS4">[[Darth Vader and the Cry of Shadows 4|''Darth Vader and the Cry of Shadows'' 4]]</ref>

The Grand Army was finally mobilized in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], approximately ten years after it was created. However, because of the rapid nature of the army's deployment, many—if not all—of the ''Cuy'val Dar'' sergeants were unable to say their farewells to the commandos they had trained, some of whom never returned from the battle on [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]]. With the Kamino contract over once the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] had broken out between the Republic and the Separatist [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], nearly all of the ''Cuy'val Dar'' went their separate ways, returning to the lives they left almost a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] prior, and doing so with a significant sum of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for their rendered services.<ref name="TZ" /> Kal Skirata, though, managed to secure himself a role as military [[Advisor/Legends|advisor]] to the Grand Army's [[Special Operations Brigade/Legends|Special Operations Brigade]] as part of a deal with Jedi Master [[Iri Camas]], and later [[Director of Special Forces]], [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Arligan Zey]]. One non-Mandalorian Cuy'val Dar sergeant named [[Kligson/Legends|Kligson]] was itching to join the fight and enlisted in the Grand Army of the Republic. As one of the Republic's rare non-clone officers, Kligson fought on the frontlines at [[Battle of Sluis Van (Clone Wars)|Sluis Van]] and [[Battle of Praesitlyn|Praesitlyn]].<ref name="EGWAC8">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 8: Tales of the Clone Wars}}</ref> By the [[21 BBY/Legends|second year]] of the war, Walon Vau had also returned to the employ of the Republic, even taking part in a [[Mission to Coruscant (Clone Wars)|strike]] against [[Jabiimi]] [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorists]] operating on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="TZ" /> As time went on, Vau also became involved in the cross-training of standard clone troopers with commando skills, along with the re-employed ''Cuy'val Dar'' sergeants Mij Gilamar and [[Wad'e Tay'haai]].<ref name="O66" />

Members of the ''Cuy'val Dar'' were also employed in the training of clones on Kamino for a second time, as part of the [[ARC program/Legends|enhanced training program]] instituted by ARC trooper [[Alpha (Advanced Recon Commando)|Alpha-17]]. During the course of the training, Cort Davin was responsible for imbuing [[Clone Commander/Legends|Clone Commander]] [[Bacara/Legends|Bacara]] of the [[Galactic Marines/Legends|Galactic Marines]] with the traditions and culture of the Journeyman Protectors,<ref name="GGAR" /> while Mandalorian trainers passed on elements of their culture to [[Clone Captain/Legends|Clone Captain]] [[Rex/Legends|Rex]], awarding the soldier the right to wear [[Jaig eyes|''jaig'' eyes]] on his [[Phase I clone trooper armor/Legends|armor]].<ref name="NewBattlefronts">''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: New Battlefronts: The Visual Guide]]''</ref> The Mandalorian [[Hunting/Legends|huntress]], [[Vhonte Tervho]], imparted her skills with dual [[DC-17 hand blaster/Legends|DC-17 blasters]] to more than a thousand up and coming [[Clone Commander/Legends|clone commanders]].<ref name="Gunslingers">{{SW|url=news/so-uncivilized-great-gunslingers-in-star-wars-part-1|series=So Uncivilized: Great Gunslingers in Star Wars|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref> 

[[File:Kal_Skirata.jpg|thumb|left|200px|''Cuy'val Dar'' sergeant [[Kal Skirata]]]]
Several members of the ''Cuy'val Dar'', all of whom were of Mandalorian origin, continued to work together in the years after the Kamino contract was finished in a secret and personal mission aimed at aiding the clones they had trained. Under the unofficial leadership of Kal Skirata, former ''Cuy'val Dar'' trainers Walon Vau and Rav Bralor attempted to obtain a means of halting the accelerated aging that plagued the clones by kidnapping Kaminoan [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Ko Sai/Legends|Ko Sai]]. Kamino's former [[Chief Scientist/Legends|Chief Scientist]] defiantly took her own life at Skirata's [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]] home, [[Kyrimorut/Legends|Kyrimorut]]—a location Bralor had secured for Skirata as a hideaway, and as a refuge for any clone who wished to desert the Grand Army in favor of freedom.<ref name="TC" /> Fellow ''Cuy'val Dar'' sergeant and doctor Mij Gilamar attempted to continue what research they had gained from Ko Sai prior to her death, in addition to acquiring further research from other sources such as [[Arkanian Microtechnologies]] with the help of the [[Null-class Advanced Recon Commando|''Null''-class ARC troopers]], before the group abducted the Separatist [[Geneticist/Legends|geneticist]] [[Ovolot Qail Uthan]], and convinced her to aid them in their quest;<ref name="O66" /> the endeavor eventually bore fruit, and a cure for the clones' accelerated aging was found and administered.<ref name="Sacrifice">''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''</ref> Skirata, Gilamar, Vau, and Bralor, along with their former ''Cuy'val Dar'' comrade Wad'e Tay'haai, spent a good deal of time at Kyrimorut in the [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] and [[Standard month/Legends|months]] following the [[Declaration of a New Order|transformation]] of the Republic into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], under the newly crowned [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. While Skirata his comrades attempted to distance themselves from the Empire and its [[City of Bone|presence]] on Mandalore, the former ''Cuy'val Dar'' trainers [[Dred Priest]] and [[Isabet Reau]]—now members of the renegade [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]]—allied themselves with the Empire as part of Death Watch [[Secret Mandalore|leader]] [[Lorka Gedyc]]'s plan to strengthen the Mandalorians. Gilamar later killed Priest in the Mandalorian [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]] after his Death Watch ties became known.<ref name="ImpCom501st" />

Many years later, during the [[25 ABY/Legends|first year]] of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic Intelligence Service|Intellegence]] [[Agent/Legends|agent]] and [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Knight [[Kubariet]] met with [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] on the planet [[Vorpa'ya]] in the [[Mandalore sector/Legends|Mandalore sector]]. There, Kubariet reminded Fett of the ''Cuy'val Dar'' formed by his father, Jango, and suggested he form a new group of ''Cuy'val Dar'' from his most [[Mandalorian Supercommando|experienced commandos]] in order to train planetary militias to resist the [[Yuuzhan Vong]].<ref name="APM">''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Cuy'val Dar'' were first referenced in the [[2004]] novel ''[[Republic Commando: Hard Contact]]'', by [[Karen Traviss]]. There, their role as trainers to the clone commandos hand-picked by Jango Fett was first established.<ref name="HC" /> ''Cuy'val Dar'' member Walon Vau made cameo appearances in the loading screens of the [[2005]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Republic Commando]]'', which ''Hard Contact'' was originally written to tie in with.<ref name="RepCom" /> The ''Cuy'val Dar'' would make their first true appearance in Traviss' short story ''[[Omega Squad: Targets]]'', and her second novel entry in the [[Republic Commando|''Republic Commando'' series]], ''[[Republic Commando: Triple Zero]]'', with the appearance of sergeants Kal Skirata and Walon Vau as full-fledged characters.<ref name="TZ" /> Members of the ''Cuy'val Dar'' appeared in Traviss' subsequent novels ''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]'',<ref name="TC" /> ''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]'',<ref name="O66" /> and ''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''.<ref name="ImpCom501st" /> The ''Cuy'val Dar'' was also mentioned in Traviss' [[EBook|e-novella]], ''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]''.<ref name="APM" />

"[[Clone Cadets]]," the first episode of the [[The Clone Wars: Season Three|third season]] of the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series, introduced the characters of [[El-Les/Legends|El-Les]] and [[Bric/Legends|Bric]], a pair of mercenary bounty hunters employed by the Republic in the training of clone troops on Kamino, without the indication of being related to Mandalore or the concept of the ''Cuy'val Dar''.

In her blog, Karen Traviss noted that "[the ''Cuy'val Dar''] either needed the fee very badly, or they were insanely loyal to Jango, or they wanted to disappear for reasons best known to themselves...and so on."
